turns.content: Gillespie quispel made a joke that I always forget the exact wording of. Something like alchemy is the gnostic yoga of the west

The quote you're thinking of is attributed to Gilles Quispel, a prominent scholar of Gnosticism, and goes something like this: "Alchemy is the yoga of the West, the Gnostic way to salvation." This aphorism encapsulates Quispel's view of alchemy as a Western counterpart to the spiritual disciplines of the East, such as yoga, with its transformative process mirroring the Gnostic emphasis on spiritual liberation and inner enlightenment. It highlights the parallels between the mystical practices of Western Hermeticism and the spiritual methodologies of other traditions. Let me know if you'd like more context or exploration of this idea!
Cab you cite a source and tell me more about the quote
